name: ATLAS urolithin A middle-aged adult trial
summary: A randomized placebo-controlled trial testing 500 mg or 1000 mg daily urolithin A for 4 months in untrained overweight middle-aged adults.
population: 90 untrained overweight middle-aged adults randomized to placebo, 500 mg urolithin A, or 1000 mg urolithin A in the registry; 88 were represented in the published analysis

trial_details.horizon_note: Results are already represented by PMID 35584623; ClinicalTrials.gov has no posted results section.
trial_details.why_it_matters: Results matter for Microbiome Metabolite Restoration, Selective Autophagy and Organelle Quality Control, and Mitophagy Enhancers because they can show whether the intervention changes function and biomarkers in middle-aged adults. That is still narrower than proof of broad aging reversal.
